hallucinations.description: The paper includes several fabricated citations with future publication years and invalid arXiv IDs (e.g., from late 2025 and 2026), indicating they were hallucinated by the model.
hallucinations.evidence: References [4] and [8] (arXiv:2509.13813), [7] (Year 2026, arXiv:2601.20026), and [9] (arXiv:2511.16275) are nonexistent.

hallucinations.description: The paper claims to provide a class-conditional coverage guarantee (Type I error control specifically for factually correct outputs). However, the mathematical formula provided for the conformal threshold computes the quantile over the entire calibration set, which would only provide marginal coverage. To achieve the claimed conditional coverage, the quantile must be computed exclusively over the nonconformity scores of the factually correct calibration samples.
hallucinations.evidence: The formula \\hat{q} = \\text{Quantile}\\left(\\{\\mathcal{R}(\\mathbf{x}_i, \\mathbf{y}_i)\\}_{i=1}^{n};\\ \\frac{\\lceil (n+1)(1-\\alpha) \\rceil}{n}\\right) computes the threshold over all n samples, contradicting the claim \\Pr\\left[\\mathcal{R}(\\mathbf{x}_{\\text{test}}, \\mathbf{y}_{\\text{test}}) \\leq \\hat{q} \\mid z_{\\text{test}} = 1\\right] \\geq 1 - \\alpha.

hallucinations.description: The paper claims that its adaptive risk-sensitive thresholding maintains conformal guarantees. However, the underlying code implementation for adaptive thresholding (`get_threshold`) incorrectly computes the quantile over the entire calibration set rather than filtering for just the correct samples. This implementation flaw breaks the theoretical guarantees claimed in the paper for the adaptive setting.
hallucinations.evidence: In `uncertainty.py`, the `get_threshold` method computes the threshold using `correct_scores = self.calibration_scores_` (which contains all scores) instead of filtering for correct samples, despite the inline comment explicitly stating `# Recompute on calibration scores of correct samples`.

overall_assessment: The paper contains several clear hallucinations. It includes fabricated citations with future dates. Additionally, there is a significant mathematical error in the formulation of the conformal prediction threshold, which contradicts the claimed class-conditional coverage guarantee. This error is further reflected in the code's adaptive thresholding implementation, which fails to filter for correct samples, thereby breaking the theoretical guarantees claimed in the text.
